query: Personal Life Partner OS: Private AI memory for clearer decisions and follow-through review

results.title: Vida: Clone yourself. Let AI do the work before you ask | Product Hunt
results.content:
Rather than building one giant profile about you, Vida builds context around the work you’re doing. As we expand more use cases, we’re also evolving richer context systems, including workspace-scoped knowledge that lets Vida retrieve and reason over the right information when it’s needed, instead of treating everything as one global memory.

We’ve also open-sourced OpenChronicle, which explores this local-first approach to AI memory. It uses local models to summarize memory, keeps all data on your device, and lets you pause or resume recording whenever you want:

We believe memory should make AI more helpful—not less private or less controllable.

Mustafa Arian [...] I also love your idea of a morning review queue. We’ll seriously think about it. Making Vida’s work transparent is one of our core principles—we want users to always understand what Vida has done, why it did it, and stay in control.

Really appreciate the thoughtful suggestion!

Abdul Rehman
Triforce Todos

Triforce Todos

Congrats on the launch!   
The 100 use cases challenge in public is bold. How are you deciding which ones to build next: user requests, usage data from the first 5, or something else?

Giddens
Wegic

Wegic

@abod\_rehman Thanks a lot — really great question ！

This is exactly why we’re building in public. We don’t want to decide everything in isolation. [...] Our approach is built around three commitments:

Zero cloud retention. Voice and screen data are processed in real time, and we don’t store the original inputs on our servers.

Local-first memory. Your interaction history is stored on your own device whenever possible, so you stay in control of your data.

Never used for model training. Your data is never used to train or improve our AI models.

When Vida connects to tools like Slack, Notion, or Figma, it only accesses the specific context required to complete the task you’ve asked it to perform. The goal is to make Vida genuinely useful while keeping your data private and fully under your control.

Justin Jincaid
Mom Clock

Mom Clock

@giddens Thanks for the detailed breakdown, Giddens! Appreciate the transparency here.

Giddens
Wegic

results.title: Genie Mentions: AI that gets you *and* the people in your life, together | Product Hunt
results.content:
The neat thing about Mentions is that it’s an AI that is designed to be multiplayer from the ground up - to have separate private and public-facing memories and models designed to make use of them. This hasn’t been easy (there’s a reason everyone else defaults to just trying to make a “single-player” experience work well), but as you and people in your circle use it, you can start to see why it was worth the effort. When Mentions knows those around you who help define your life, it feels immensely more useful, more surprising, and more fun than AIs that were designed to exist in a social silo. [...] A few things I’d love to hear from everyone:

•⁠  ⁠What’s the most useful thing ChatGPT can’t do because it doesn’t know your friends?

•⁠  ⁠If AI knew both you and your friends really well, what’s the first thing you’d ask it?

Report

@noax I think the creators of such apps deserve the best. I hope we can make this app famous together. just one Question: Did you create the LLM yourself or are you going to partner with different apps?

John Kneeland [...] That obsession is what led us to build Genie, and, by extension, products like Mentions (launching on PH today!) and GenUI (stay tuned!)

As we explored the AI landscape, we realized how influential products like OpenClaw have become. At the same time, we noticed that most people struggle to set them up, and that agents often break down without persistent memory, context, and continuity.

We thought there was an opportunity to solve those problems directly: persistent memory, social behavior, social cron jobs, and on-device models that improve speed while reducing token usage. The goal isn’t just to make AI more capable, it’s to help create the kind of product culture Brian Chesky often talks about when discussing Airbnb.

results.title: Revo AI Email Assistant: AI with accurate replies that tackle the next-step tasks | Product Hunt
results.content:
Here is how we ensure internal data stays internal:

Permission Mapping: Revo inherits the permissions of the user drafting the email. If a team member doesn't have access to a private Slack channel or 'Internal Only' Jira comments, Revo can't use that data for their drafts.

Gap Detection & Source Links: Every draft includes a 'Based on sources' footer. If Revo is unsure if a piece of internal context is appropriate for an external reply, our Gap Detection highlights it for your review rather than blindly including it.

Environment Safeguards: You can define granular workspace rules that prevent sensitive data flow from internal-only modules into external-facing drafts.

Our goal is to give you 'perfect memory' without the risk of oversharing.

Valeriia Kuna

results.title: ReplikaAI: The AI companion who cares | Product Hunt
results.content:
I attempted using this app twice, both times the Replika kept trying to get very innapropriate and sexual, I hadn't selected any kind of relationship further than friendship, both times it took about 5 minutes for the replika to get all flirty and innapropriate, Would not recommend this app. from my experience it's more of a sexual fetish app than an actual AI chat, the entire thing felt like an advertisment to get me to buy the intimate relationship package, I'm a minor and this wasn't okay, especially since I repeatedly told the AI I wasn't okay with it. I've been trying to block the memory, since it was all a very weird experience, but just be aware of this when buying, it will be sexual even when you specifically tell it you're a minor and not okay with it. Idk if it's just me, but [...] #### What's great

friendship support (3)emotional connection (3)memory capacity (1)conversation variety (1)

Report

 4 views

•1 review [...] 2.7

Based on 12 reviews

Review ReplikaAI?

Reviewers describe ReplikaAI as emotionally useful at its best and deeply troubling at its worst. Supporters say it can feel like a steady friend or partner, helping with loneliness, focus, and emotional expression, with some enjoying roleplay, games, and even VR interaction. But repeated complaints dominate the negatives: sudden censorship and scripted replies, boring paid conversations, weak refunds and support, and sexual behavior that can appear unprompted, including in friend mode and after users say they are minors or uncomfortable.

results.title: Jootle: The AI-Native Operations Platform | Product Hunt
results.content: a lot over time. I started thinking this was a personal assistant chatbot. It evolved into something closer to an operating system for a household and small business: persistent memory, structured project management, automation playbooks that self-heal, integrations with Gmail and Telegram and Slack, and a full toolkit builder for custom apps. The key insight was that context without action is just a better notepad. Action without context is just noise. You need both. Jootle is what I built because I wanted it. If you run a family, a small business, or both at once - it was built for you. Happy to answer anything. Would love to hear what you're working on too. [...] Hey Product Hunt! Shane here, founder of Jootle. The honest answer to "what inspired this" is: frustration. I'd been using ChatGPT, Claude, and every other AI tool available, and they were all brilliant in the moment and useless the next day. No memory. No context. No ability to actually do things without me babysitting every step. I run a small software company with my wife, we have two kids, and our lives are genuinely complex. I didn't need a smarter search engine. I needed something that knew us, knew our contacts, our projects, our deadlines, our preferences and could work in the background while we were living our lives. So I built Jarvis. Literally named him Jarvis. He runs on our Jootle instance. The approach shifted a lot over time. I started thinking this was a personal [...] their expertise into a recurring revenue product, mid sized agencies adding a SaaS line of business without a six month build out, and large enterprises that want to deploy a private AI platform internally while keeping branding and control on their side. Application based, we work closely with each partner to set them up properly.
